3A_Bibliographic.citation: Lichenstein, S. D., Reardon, T., Engel, C., & Potenza, M. N. (2021). Identification and validation of distinct latent neurodevelopmental profiles in the Adolescent Brain and Cognitive Development Study. Biological Psychiatry: Cognitive Neuroscience and Neuroimaging, 6(12), 1245-1255.
3A_Bibliographic.journal: Biological Psychiatry: Cognitive Neuroscience and Neuroimaging
3A_Bibliographic.notes: This paper is about latent neurodevelopmental profiles identified via fMRI task-based brain activation (LPA of 9 ROIs across 3 fMRI tasks). Screen time appears only as a descriptive variable in Supplementary Table S3 comparing profiles. Screen time is NOT the primary exposure or outcome. This paper likely does not fit the systematic review's focus on screen time/social media.

3B_Sample_Data.verbatim_sample_description: A total of 7,789 participants out of the 11,875 had complete fMRI data on all three tasks and were included in subsequent analyses... due to incorrect post-processing of fMRI data acquired on Philips scanners in ABCD Release 2.0, participants scanned on a Philips MRI scanner (1,031 out of the 7,789 with complete data) were excluded, resulting in a final sample of 6,758 individuals.

3C_Exposure.exposure_variable: N/A - Primary analysis is latent profile analysis of fMRI brain activation patterns across 3 tasks (MID, SST, EN-back)
3C_Exposure.exposure_instrument: fMRI task-based brain activation (9 ROIs: NAcc, mOFC, rACC for MID; IFG, cACC, thalamus for SST; dlPFC, insula, amygdala for EN-back)
3C_Exposure.modalities: N/A - not a screen time study
3C_Exposure.verbatim_exposure_description: N/A - This paper uses fMRI activation as indicators for latent profile analysis, not screen time as an exposure.
3C_Exposure.notes: Screen time is only used as a descriptive variable in supplementary comparisons across profiles. It is NOT the primary exposure.

3D_Outcome.primary_outcome: Latent neurodevelopmental profiles (7 profiles identified) and their associations with demographic, clinical, and behavioral variables
3D_Outcome.outcome_instrument: fMRI task-based activation (MID, SST, EN-back tasks)
3D_Outcome.verbatim_outcome_description: The primary outcome is the identification and validation of distinct latent neurodevelopmental profiles based on task-fMRI brain activation patterns.
3D_Outcome.notes: Screen time appears only in Supplementary Table S3 as a descriptive comparison across profiles (F=6.80, p<.0001, d=0.36). It is not a primary outcome.

3E_Design_Analysis.model_type_detail: Latent profile analysis (LPA) with split-half validation; post-hoc comparisons using ANOVA and chi-square tests with Bonferroni correction
3E_Design_Analysis.covariates_listed: head motion (covariate in LPA); sex, race, ethnicity, scanner type controlled in sensitivity analyses
3E_Design_Analysis.verbatim_analysis_description: Latent profile analyses were conducted using Mplus. The full sample was randomly split into a model training sample (n = 3,379) and a model testing sample (n = 3,379). LPAs were conducted in the training sample and the optimal solution was then applied to the testing sample for validation.

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Screen time (sum of weekday+weekend hours). F=6.80 across 7 profiles.
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: Supplementary Table S3: Screen time F=6.80, p<.0001, d=0.36

3G_Reporting_Quality.abstract_significance_verbatim: N/A - abstract does not discuss screen time
3G_Reporting_Quality.notes: Screen time is not a focus of this paper. It only appears in supplementary profile comparisons.

3H_Recommendations.notes: No recommendations about screen time or social media.

3I_Rhetorical_Moves.discussion_characterization: This paper is primarily about identifying distinct neurodevelopmental profiles based on fMRI task activation in the ABCD study. Screen time is mentioned only briefly as one of many descriptive variables that differ across profiles. The discussion focuses on the neurodevelopmental profiles and their clinical implications for psychiatric vulnerability, not on screen time.
3I_Rhetorical_Moves.ambiguity_notes: This paper does not primarily examine screen time as exposure or outcome. Screen time appears only in supplementary descriptive comparisons. Should likely be excluded from a review focused on screen time/social media effects.
